quote_of_day.text: Having a sacred animal is the most retarded thing a culture could do. Eating beef is based, it's just a fucking animal. If you think it's a god get your malnutritioned brain checked. >muh milk Every other mammal gives milk, why is cow special.
quote_of_day.name: Ambedkar
quote_of_day.topic: critique of sacred animals, dietary prohibitions

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Narthie

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A high-pressure system of beef-eating debates dominated the morning, with a front of sacrilegious comments sparking a heated response. Anon traded barbs over the cultural significance of cows, with one respondent calling it 'retarded' to consider them sacred.

A scattered drizzle of pop culture discussion emerged in the afternoon, with a thread on potential Indian idol groups drawing some interest. Anon joked about an all-female group and their potential singing and dancing abilities.

A cold snap of Chan culture advocacy hit the board late in the evening, with one poster pushing for its adoption in India. The discussion was brief but pointed, with no clear resolution.

A series of short, sharp exchanges characterised the day's discourse climate.

articles.title: Eating Beef Sparks Debate on Faith, Culture
articles.body:
Ambedkar kicked off the discussion by calling the concept of a sacred animal 'retarded,' stating that eating beef is a normal practice and that cows are just animals.

He criticized the idea that cows are considered divine, suggesting that those who hold this belief should get their 'malnutritioned brain checked.' Anon responded by arguing that the disagreement stems from different frameworks and moral layers that people assume are relevant to each other.

Another anon chimed in, stating that cows are not considered God, but rather a protected entity in the context of Aghniya.

The conversation took a turn when Anon discussed the genetic differences between the X and Y chromosomes, debunking the myth that the Y chromosome is weaker.

Narthie expressed frustration with the thread, stating that it was initially decent but got ruined by a few users, including the OP, who brought up unrelated topics.

Anon discussed the idea of starting a new branch of faith, citing the example of Buddhism, and argued that if one wants to stick to their faith, they should not abandon it easily.

The discussion also touched on the topic of genetics and the idea of a 'genetic bottleneck.' Haggu argued that certain populations, such as Ashkenazis, have exceptional qualities due to their genetic history.

Anon proposed recreating the Indus Valley Diet, which includes meat and seafood, millets, and buffalo milk, and discussed the potential physiological consequences of this diet. The thread also saw several exchanges on the topic of faith, culture, and the offense caused by certain practices.

Soythie compared the offense caused by cow slaughter to the offense caused by throwing pork at a mosque. Dyaush argued that the sanctity of cows emerged independently of their utility, citing the example of the protected rooster.

Overall, the discussion revolved around the intersection of faith, culture, and personal choices, with various users sharing their perspectives on the matter.

articles.title: Anon Discusses Potential Indian Idol Group
articles.body:
Ambedkar started a thread asking if readers' sisters were auditioning for an Indian idol group called 'gobarjeeta kumari girl group.' The OP wondered if the group would fail like DEL48 or if Koreans were better than Japanese at making international branches.

Dyaush and others responded with enthusiasm, with Dyaush expressing support for Northeastern Indians and appreciation for their appearance. Dyaush also mentioned fond memories of a Nepali vendor. Some respondents made graphic and misogynistic comments about female idols.

Mococo fantasized about dating and having sex with an idol. Haggu suggested that an idol like Rushia needed to be 'fucked like a whore' to be 'okay.' Lungi described a hypothetical scenario involving an unattractive Indian idol.

Ozymandias joked about creating a sexually suggestive environment for minors. Motivanon expressed disappointment if the group wasn't made up of Northeastern or North Indian women. The thread also touched on the topic of voting systems and fandom.

Gura suggested a voting system similar to 4chan's. Bhondu urged patience, noting that the group hadn't even formed yet.

articles.title: Pushing Chan Culture in India
articles.body:
Soyjeet kicked off the discussion by stating that Chan culture needs to be pushed in India to attract genuine female incels, as opposed to those found on Reddit.

Anon Bhondu responded, claiming that female incels, or 'femcels,' are largely driven by hormones and upbringing rather than genetics.

Another anon, Chuddie, made several inflammatory posts, including one where he suggested that women want to be raped and that he would make feminist page admins pregnant if he could stop time.

Momojeet countered that it's not possible to attract many women to imageboards, citing his own experiences with girls who have used 4chan.

Chuddie continued to make provocative statements, including one where he claimed that women are inferior men and that the Y chromosome is what makes men superior.

Momojeet also noted that many girls who use imageboards are either lesbian or bisexual, and that straight girls who are aware of 4chan often 'larp' on Instagram.

Haggu chimed in, suggesting that Indian men, or 'jeetas,' can't understand incel humour and take it literally.

Chuddie also claimed that women who make sense on imageboards are actually men, and that women have never contributed unless they can rely on their bodies or attention-seeking behaviour.

articles.title: Fuwawa warns against replying to spammer
articles.body: Fuwawa urged fellow posters not to reply to a spammer. The spammer was active on the board, posting content from another imageboard. Fuwawa explained that ignoring the spammer would cause the thread to auto-delete, rather than requiring manual deletion. Other posters, including Nepajeet and Gura, chimed in with comments and cryptic remarks.

articles.title: Anon Questions Army Service, Others Respond
articles.body: Soyjeet asked on /b/ what stops an anonymous poster from joining the Indian Army's XV Corps, participating in counter-insurgency operations in Kashmir, and posting a photo as proof. One respondent, Ozymandias, dismissed the idea, saying the poster would end up taking bullets and setting up medical camps for "ungrateful fuckers
